# Cold War Vocabulary — Human-Readable Appendix

This appendix lists the domain-specific terms used to assess whether LDA
topics capture Cold-War-relevant content.  Terms are grouped into five
semantic categories.  Each entry includes a brief source annotation
explaining its relevance to the JFK assassination records.

## Geopolitical

| Term | Source annotation |
|---|---|
| cuba | Primary locus of 1962 missile crisis and DGI operations. |
| soviet | Adjective form used pervasively in CIA/FBI reporting. |
| russia | Geographic reference in diplomatic and intelligence cables. |
| mexico | Oswald's documented visit to Mexico City, Sept–Oct 1963. |
| vietnam | Concurrent Cold War theatre referenced in policy documents. |
| germany | Divided state central to Cold War geopolitics. |
| berlin | Focal point of East–West tension (Wall, airlift, stations). |

## Agency

| Term | Source annotation |
|---|---|
| cia | Central Intelligence Agency — primary producing agency for JFK files. |
| fbi | Federal Bureau of Investigation — domestic intelligence and Oswald file. |
| kgb | Soviet Committee for State Security — foreign counterpart. |
| nsa | National Security Agency — signals intelligence references. |
| dgi | Cuban Directorate of Intelligence — Cuban counterpart. |

## Tradecraft

| Term | Source annotation |
|---|---|
| surveillance | Physical and technical monitoring operations. |
| agent | Human intelligence asset or officer. |
| source | Human intelligence provider (distinct from officer). |
| operation | Planned intelligence activity or covert action. |
| covert | Modifier for clandestine activities. |
| defector | Individual who changes allegiance across blocs. |
| handler | Officer managing a human intelligence source. |

## Diplomatic

| Term | Source annotation |
|---|---|
| embassy | Diplomatic mission — site of intelligence activity. |
| consulate | Consular office — Oswald visited Soviet and Cuban consulates. |
| ambassador | Senior diplomatic representative. |
| defection | Act of changing allegiance (noun form). |
| asylum | Political protection sought by defectors. |
| communist | Ideological descriptor in intelligence reporting. |
| party | Political party — often Communist Party references. |

## Named Actor

| Term | Source annotation |
|---|---|
| oswald | Lee Harvey Oswald — central subject of JFK assassination files. |
| ruby | Jack Ruby — killed Oswald; subject of extensive investigation. |
| castro | Fidel Castro — Cuban leader; subject of CIA operations. |
| khrushchev | Nikita Khrushchev — Soviet premier during key Cold War events. |
